narrative: PRL kept bleeding from the rally — volume-weighted price settled at $0.7909 (-16.43% 24h) across seven Ethereum pairs as cross-venue volume cooled another 32% to $189.9k. The drawdown didn't bite the bridge, though: custody climbed +13.6% to 450,579 PRL and minted WPRL +15.5% to 449,062, pulling surplus down to a near-zero 0.34% as the queue keeps clearing into canonical supply. Network hashrate gave back some ground at 23.07 EH/s (-16% in 24h), with block times easing to ~120s and MinePRL pool GPUs cooling 58 → 45 — the first capacity dip of the cycle, worth watching but not yet directional. Code velocity stayed strong (public-frontend 87 commits/7d shipping RC5.33 stats redesign, PearlWallet 10/7d, L1 core picked up +14 stars to 153).

market.prl_circulating_supply_method: block_height × current_block_reward (constant-subsidy approximation — Pearl is in month 1, before the first halving)
sources: dexscreener:tokens/0x07696DcaB55E62cfef953666b29Fe1970518cB00 (7 Ethereum pairs aggregated, volume-weighted price and 24h change)

network.active_workers_scope: MinePRL pool only — network-wide miner count is not exposed by Pearl RPC

anomalies: PRL price -16.43% in 24h ($0.9181 → $0.7909), continuing the post-rally drawdown on cooling volume ($280.7k → $189.9k). No bridge or infrastructure event — flows still net-inbound through the move.
Bridge custody +13.6% to 450,579 PRL and minted WPRL +15.5% to 449,062 — the mint queue caught up: surplus tightened from 1.97% to 0.34%.
Network hashrate -16.3% to 23.07 EH/s — first 24h capacity decline of the cycle; MinePRL pool GPUs 58 → 45 (-22%), pool share down to 0.033%. Worth watching but not yet a trend.

social.summary: X API not exercised this run. Spot continues to correct (-16% on the day) while bridge custody keeps making new highs — neutral read pending a sampled-tweets pass.

github.last_commit_msg: fix(rpc): use chain.IsCurrent for getblocktemplate IBD check (#125)
feat(stats): at-a-glance Inflow / Outflow / Net + visible live-refresh (RC5.33)
docs: tidy mirror references and audit attributions
